title: Unit 3 – Check-in
description: The Emerald Isle – Irland, Klima, Geschichte

vocab.ex: Hm, I don't think I'm the right shape for those jeans – but look, these ones would fit me well.
We've got a new neighbour who I think will really fit in well here.
This year we're going to go on holiday in the winter instead of the summer.
If it's sunny tomorrow, we'll have our picnic in the Welsh countryside. If it rains, a castle visit might be a good alternative.
Is that an emerald in your necklace? It matches your green eyes.
Hamburg is in the northern part of Germany.
What's the climate like in New York? They have very hot summers and very cold winters with lots of snow.
Tourism is an important part of the economy in famous cities like London or Paris.
In rich countries with lots of food for everyone, it can be hard to even imagine a famine.
What are the Irish famous for? I hear they're very friendly.
I have lots of plants in my living room but it isn't as nice as a garden.
Hundreds of years ago, diseases killed many more people than today.
A huge wave hit the island and wiped out some of the villages.
There's been too much rain and not enough sun; that isn't good for the crops.
Where does this path lead?
You write about too many different things in your report; you should find a clear focus.
Germany is famous for the production of expensive cars.
Without the help of modern medicine, many more people would die of diseases.
Frankfurt is the financial centre of Germany.
The western part of Ireland has some mountains.
Ireland doesn't get very hot or very cold weather; it's mild there.
India has been independent since 1947, but for many years before that it was a British colony.
Scotland, Wales and Northern Ireland aren't independent nations. They're part of the UK.
The western and eastern parts of Germany were reunited in 1990.
Before 1990, there was West Berlin and East Berlin. But now the two parts are a single city.
I'm afraid he died a very violent death; it was murder.
A lot of people die in the many violent conflicts in the world today.
Rome is the centre of the Catholic religion.
Bad crops, disease and also long, violent conflicts are all reasons why people in some countries still die of starvation today.
Not every beach has sand. Many are rocky.
Clover is a plant you can find all over Ireland. Cows eat it.
All shamrocks are clovers, but not all clovers are shamrocks.
What kind of scenery do you find beautiful? I like rugged scenery, so places with mountains are the best.
I don't want to go where all the tourists go, I want to eat where the locals eat.
This is my favourite district. It's where there's a lot of art on the walls.
These buskers can really sing. Let's give them some money.
